testing_centers.notes: In-person test centers worldwide and online proctored via OnVUE

id_requirements: Two forms of ID are required. Primary ID must be government-issued, include candidate's name, photo, and signature (e.g. passport, driver's license). Secondary ID must include candidate's name and signature or name and photo.
exam_rules: No notes, scratch paper, electronic devices, books, or study materials permitted. Personal items must be stored. No food or drink except water. Restroom breaks allowed but timer continues. Testing center staff may monitor via video/audio.
renewal_options: Earn CE credits through CompTIA CE program (training, conferences, publishing, teaching, higher certs) or retake the current exam version before expiry.
target_audience: Incident response analyst, security operations center (SOC) analyst
recommended_experience: Network+, Security+, or equivalent knowledge, with a minimum of 4 years of hands-on experience as an incident response analyst, security operations center (SOC) analyst, or equivalent experience

retake_policy.notes: No waiting period required before first retake. After the second failed attempt, candidates must wait 14 calendar days before any subsequent attempt. No limit on total attempts.

objectives.title: System and network architecture
sub_objectives: Explaining log ingestion, operating system (OS) concepts, infrastructure, network architecture, identity and access management (IAM), encryption, and sensitive data protection

objectives.title: Malicious activity indicators
sub_objectives: Analyzing network anomalies like bandwidth spikes and rogue devices, host issues like unauthorized software and data exfiltration, application irregularities like unexpected communication and service interruptions, and threats like social engineering attacks

objectives.title: Tools and techniques
sub_objectives: Detecting malicious activity using tools like Wireshark, security information and event management (SIEM), and VirusTotal, along with techniques like pattern recognition and email analysis, supported by scripting languages like Python and PowerShell

objectives.title: Threat intelligence and hunting
sub_objectives: Comparing threat actors, tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTP); confidence levels; collection methods; intelligence sharing; and hunting techniques

objectives.title: Process improvement
sub_objectives: Standardizing processes, streamlining operations, integrating tools, and using a single pane of glass

objectives.title: Vulnerability scanning
sub_objectives: Implementing asset discovery, internal vs. external scanning, agent vs. agentless, credentialed vs. non-credentialed, passive vs. active, static vs. dynamic, and critical infrastructure scanning

objectives.title: Assessment tool output
sub_objectives: Analyzing network scanning, web application scanners, vulnerability scanners, debuggers, multipurpose tools, and cloud infrastructure assessments

objectives.title: Vulnerability prioritization
sub_objectives: Interpreting common vulnerability scoring system (CVSS), validating findings, assessing exploitability, and considering asset value and zero-day vulnerabilities

objectives.title: Mitigation controls
sub_objectives: Recommending controls for cross-site scripting (XSS), overflow vulnerabilities, and data poisoning

objectives.title: Vulnerability response
sub_objectives: Explaining compensating controls, patching, configuration management, maintenance windows, exceptions, governance, service-level objectives (SLOs), secure software development life cycle (SDLC), and threat modeling

objectives.title: Attack methodology frameworks
sub_objectives: Explaining cyber kill chains, diamond model of intrusion analysis, MITRE ATT&CK, Open Source Security Testing Methodology Manual (OSSTMM), and OWASP testing guide

objectives.title: Incident response activities
sub_objectives: Performing detection, analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery

objectives.title: Incident management life cycle
sub_objectives: Explaining incident response plans, tools, playbooks, tabletop exercises, training, business continuity (BC), disaster recovery (DR), forensic analysis, and root cause analysis

objectives.title: Vulnerability management reporting
sub_objectives: Explaining compliance reports, action plans, inhibitors to remediation, metrics, key performance indicators (KPIs), and stakeholder communication

objectives.title: Incident response reporting
sub_objectives: Explaining incident declaration, escalation, reporting, communication, root cause analysis, lessons learned, and metrics and KPIs

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Learn
official_study_resources.notes: Official e-learning with performance-based questions and study guides

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Practice
official_study_resources.notes: Adaptive practice question engine with rationale
